title: Litany of St. Rita
description: A responsive litany to St. Rita, intercessor in difficult and impossible cases.
body:
LITANY OF ST. RITA OF CASCIA

Lord, have mercy,

Christ, have mercy,

Lord, have mercy,

Response: Have mercy on us

God the Father of Heaven,

God the Son, Redeemer of the world,

God the Holy Spirit,

Holy Trinity, one God,

Response: Pray for us

Mary Immaculate, Mother of God

Mary, Mother and comforter of those who suffer

Mary, Queen of all Saints

Mary, beloved intercessor of St. Rita

Response: Pray for us

St. Rita, gift from Heaven:

St. Rita, our powerful intercessor:

St. Rita, endowed with glory:

St. Rita, admired from your childhood:

St. Rita, lover of solitude:

St. Rita, model of purity of heart:

St. Rita, model of all gentleness:

St. Rita, model of a good wife:

St. Rita, excellent model of a devoted mother:

St. Rita, model of true forgiveness:

St. Rita, living model of self-denial:

St. Rita, mirror of religious life:

St. Rita, mirror of obedience:

St. Rita, admirable in patience:

St. Rita, hero of self-sacrifice:

St. Rita, witness in doing penance:

St. Rita, holy widow:

St. Rita, generous to the poor:

St. Rita, generous to the poor:

St. Rita, zealous in answering the holy call:

St. Rita, miraculously admitted into the convent:

St. Rita, fragrance of myrrh:

St. Rita, garden of every virtue:

St. Rita, beloved of Jesus Crucified:

St. Rita, wounded by a thorn on your forehead:

St. Rita, beloved daughter of the Virgin Mary:

St. Rita, thirsty for Divine Love:

St. Rita, clothed with glory:

St. Rita, pearl of Heaven:

St. Rita, glory of the Order of St. Augustine:

St. Rita, precious treasure of Umbria:

St. Rita, powerful in holiness:

St. Rita, relief of those in distress:

St. Rita, comforter of those who suffer:

St. Rita, anchor of salvation:

St. Rita, intercessor for the sick:

St. Rita, intercessor in impossible cases:

St. Rita, refuge of those in danger:

St. Rita, powerful help for all:

St. Rita, wonder of the world:

St. Rita, received joyfully into Heaven:

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, spare us, O Lord

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, graciously hear us, O Lord

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us

V: You have marked, O Lord, your servant Rita

R: With the seal of love and suffering

LET US PRAY:

O God, who in your unfailing goodness have been pleased to hear the intercession of your servant Rita, grant us through her intercession those things that are impossible by human strength. Have mercy on us who suffer, and help all who do not believe in you to know that you are the reward of the humble, the comforter of all who are afraid, and the strength of the weak. We ask this through Christ our Lord. Amen

title: Litany of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: A litany asking the Blessed Virgin Mary, Holy Mother of God, to intercede for us.
body:
LITANY OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY

Lord, have mercy.
Lord, have mercy.
Christ, have mercy.
Christ, have mercy.
Lord, have mercy.
Lord, have mercy.
Christ, hear us.
Christ, graciously hear us.
God the Father of Heaven, have mercy on us.
God the Son, Redeemer of the world, have mercy on us.
God the Holy Spirit, have mercy on us.
Holy Trinity, one God, have mercy on us.

Response: Pray for us

Holy Mary,
Holy Mother of God,
Holy Virgin, greatest of virgins,
Mother of Christ,
Mother of divine grace,
Most holy Mother,
Mother most pure,
Mother without stain,
Mother without sin,
Beloved Mother,
Admirable Mother,
Mother of good counsel,
Mother of the Creator,
Mother of the Redeemer,
Virgin most orderly,
Virgin most honorable,
Virgin most praised,
Virgin most powerful,
Virgin most merciful,
Faithful Virgin,
Seat of justice,
Seat of wisdom,
Cause of our joy,
Vessel of grace,
Vessel of honor,
Excellent vessel of devotion,
Mystical rose,
Tower of David,
Tower of ivory,
House of gold,
Ark of the Covenant,
Gate of Heaven,
Morning Star,
Health of the sick,
Refuge of sinners,
Comforter of the afflicted,
Help of Christians,
Queen of Angels,
Queen of Patriarchs,
Queen of Prophets,
Queen of Apostles,
Queen of Martyrs,
Queen of Confessors,
Queen of Virgins,
Queen of all Saints,
Queen conceived without original sin,
Queen assumed into Heaven,
Queen of the holy Rosary,
Queen of peace.

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, spare us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, graciously hear us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us.

V. Pray for us, O Holy Mother of God.
R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

LET US PRAY:

O Lord God, grant us your servants lasting health of soul and body. Through the intercession of holy Mary, ever Virgin, deliver us from the troubles of this present life, so that we may obtain eternal joy.
We ask this through Christ our Lord. Amen.

title: Litany of Divine Mercy
description: A litany responding to the mercy of God with trust and prayer.
body:
LITANY OF DIVINE MERCY

Lord, have mercy - Lord, have mercy.
Christ, have mercy - Christ, have mercy.
Lord, have mercy - Lord, have mercy.
Christ, hear us - Christ, graciously hear us.
God the Father of Heaven - have mercy on us.
God the Son, Redeemer of the world - have mercy on us.
God the Holy Spirit - have mercy on us.
Holy Trinity, one God - have mercy on us.

Response: We trust in you

Divine Mercy, highest attribute of our Creator.
Divine Mercy, summit of the perfection of our Savior.
Divine Mercy, immeasurable love of the Sanctifying Spirit.
Divine Mercy, unfathomable mystery of the Holy Trinity.
Divine Mercy, sign of the supreme power of Almighty God.
Divine Mercy, revealed in the creation of the angelic spirits in Heaven.
Divine Mercy, which created us and commanded us to exist from nothing.
Divine Mercy, which grants us immortal life.
Divine Mercy, which shields us from the punishment we deserve.
Divine Mercy, which raises us from the hardship, weakness, and pain of our sins.
Divine Mercy, which makes us righteous in the Word made Man.
Divine Mercy, which overflows from the wounds of Christ.
Divine Mercy, which flows from within the Sacred Heart of Jesus.
Divine Mercy, which gave us the Blessed Virgin Mary as Mother of Mercy.
Divine Mercy, shown in the founding of the Catholic Church.
Divine Mercy, found in the mysteries of the Holy Sacraments.
Divine Mercy, given to all humanity through the Sacraments of Baptism and Penance.
Divine Mercy, given in the Sacraments of the Eucharist and Holy Orders.
Divine Mercy, shown to us when it calls and leads us into sanctifying the righteous.
Divine Mercy, fulfilled in the perfection of holy people.
Divine Mercy, fountain of health for the sick and all who suffer.
Divine Mercy, comfort of all those with sorrowful hearts.
Divine Mercy, hope of souls troubled by despair.
Divine Mercy, constant companion everywhere for all people.
Divine Mercy, which hastens to bring us its graces.
Divine Mercy, peace of those who are dying.
Divine Mercy, refreshment and comfort of the faithful departed in purgatory.
Divine Mercy, heavenly joy for all the saints.
Divine Mercy, crown of all the saints.
Divine Mercy, unfailing fountain of miracles.

Lamb of God, who showed us your great mercy by redeeming the world through your holy cross, spare us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who offers yourself to us with love and mercy in every celebration of the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass, graciously hear us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world through your infinite mercy, have mercy on us.

Leader: Lord, have mercy.
All: Christ, have mercy, Lord, have mercy.
Leader: The tender mercies of the Lord are over all his works.
All: I will sing of the mercies of the Lord forever.

Let us pray:

O God of infinite mercy, whose treasures of goodness and love have no limit, we beg you to look upon your people here on earth and increase your mercy upon us again and again, so that it may help us never to despair, no matter how great the trials we meet, but always to turn toward and keep your commandments with hope and fulfill your holy will, which is mercy itself. We ask this through our Lord Jesus Christ, your Son, King of Peace and Mercy, who lives and reigns with you and the Holy Spirit, together showing us mercy, forever and ever. Amen.
